Cover inspect_image, parse_ext4, parse_btrfs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:17:39.170641522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:57:12Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:57:12Z","close_reason":"Extended fuzz_detect_filesystem to cover inspect_image plus deterministic parse_ext4/parse_btrfs facade consistency checks, then verified the target with remote cargo check and clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-idp4","title":"Update CHANGELOG.md to cover commits from 2026-03-21 through current","description":"## What
Update CHANGELOG.md to document all significant commits since the last entry (2026-03-21).
Approximately 60+ commits need documentation, organized by capability area (not chronological).

## Why
CHANGELOG.md covers "2026-02-09 through 2026-03-21" with 536 commits documented. Since then,
major work has landed: btrfs experimental RW support, crash-consistency matrix, fallocate
operations, log contract hardening, per-core FUSE dispatch, evidence pipeline expansion,
tracing flake fixes, and 81+ new tests across 4 crates.

The CHANGELOG serves as the project history for onboarding and as input to the
comprehensive-codebase-report skill. Stale changelog = invisible work.

## Implementation Plan
1. Run: git log --oneline --since="2026-03-21" | wc -l (count commits)
2. Group commits by capability area (following existing CHANGELOG structure):
   - On-Disk Parsing (ext4/btrfs)
   - FUSE Mount and VFS Layer
   - MVCC Concurrency Engine
   - Self-Healing Repair Pipeline
   - Write Path (Allocation, Extents, Inodes, Directories, Xattrs)
   - Journal and WAL Recovery
   - CLI and Observability
   - Conformance Harness and Testing
   - Foundation Types and Error Handling
3. Write entries with commit links: [hexsha](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenfs/commit/hexsha)
4. Update header: "Period covered: 2026-02-09 through 2026-04-06"
5. Update total commit count

## Considerations
- Follow existing style: bullet points grouped by feature area, commit links in brackets
- Don't document individual test additions — summarize as "expanded test coverage for X"
- Include the tracing callsite cache fix as it's architecturally interesting
- Include the btrfs fallocate sector-alignment fix
- Include the per-core metrics test fix
- Don't document build/fmt/cosmetic commits

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All significant commits since 2026-03-21 documented
- [ ] Organized by capability area matching existing structure
- [ ] Commit links included and correct
- [ ] Period and total count updated in header","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:33.040113984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037607885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037145379Z","close_reason":"Updated CHANGELOG.md through 2026-04-06 with 134-post-cutoff commit coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-idp4","depends_on_id":"bd-g1ue","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:11.676458110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ii3nk","title":"CONFORMANCE: freeze ext4 collapse/insert fallocate parity","description":"Queue-empty porting-to-rust fallback. Ext4 FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE and FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE are implemented in ffs-core, but FEATURE_PARITY.md only calls out ZERO_RANGE and existing executable evidence is crate-local unit coverage. Add ffs-harness conformance coverage for collapse shifting/shrinking and insert hole/growth behavior, then update FEATURE_PARITY.md so the spec-first parity record reflects the implemented contracts.","notes":"Claimed by Codex for porting-to-rust queue-empty parity fallback. Agent Mail health is red due missing schema tables, so coordination is recorded in Beads only.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:37:31.044827411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:51:43.929803844Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:51:43.927852409Z","close_reason":"Fixed ext4 COLLAPSE_RANGE EOF-reach rejection to return EINVAL, added ffs-harness conformance coverage for collapse shift/shrink, EOF rejection without mutation, and insert hole/growth behavior, updated parity evidence, and validated with fmt, diff check, targeted tests, full conformance gate, check, clippy, and UBS review. UBS reports the existing conformance.rs panic/expect inventory.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ik16s","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_detect_filesystem target","description":"ffs_core::detect_filesystem (lib.rs:177) - create new fuzz target","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:47:26.614709142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of implemented bead bd-kbe35; fuzz_detect_filesystem target now exists.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ipz8p","title":"add mounted-path FIEMAP request-flag coverage for SYNC and invalid-flag rejection","description":"Survey finding: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` already implements FIEMAP request-flag behavior for mounted ioctls: `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` triggers a pre-query fsync on writable mounts, and unsupported request flags reject with `EBADR`. Today that contract is only proven by direct `ffs-fuse` dispatch tests; `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` exercises only plain FIEMAP success paths.

Scope:
- Extend mounted-path FIEMAP helpers in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` so callers can set custom request flags and buffer sizes.
- Add an operator-path scenario that issues `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` with `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` on a writable mount and proves the ioctl either reaches userspace or soft-skips before dispatch.
- Add an operator-path scenario that issues FIEMAP with an unsupported request flag and proves stable `EBADR` rejection without mutating file state.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` if the operator-visible FIEMAP contract now includes request-flag behavior beyond plain extent reporting.

Acceptance:
- Mounted-path tests prove `SYNC` and invalid-flag FIEMAP request behavior with deterministic assertions and ioctl-trace evidence.
- Soft-skips clearly separate kernel/VFS transport limits from FrankenFS regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:28:52.771387332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:36:52.084219472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:36:52.082037210Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-iq9j2","title":"Freeze exact self-healing demo output contract","description":"Replace loose starts_with/contains assertions in crates/ffs-repair/tests/self_healing_demo_e2e.rs with a scrubbed exact golden for the representative self-healing demo output lines, then verify via rch fmt/check/clippy and targeted test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:03:58.602555325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:15:06.924995221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:15:06.921735883Z","close_reason":"Froze exact self-healing demo output contract with duration scrubbing; verified via rch repair E2E test file plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-repair","golden-artifacts","testing"]}

@@ -57,7 +57,7 @@
 | btrfs subvolume mount | `fs/btrfs/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext::subvol_objectid` configurable; `walk_btrfs_fs_tree` resolves the requested tree root. Harness coverage still proves the default root alias, and `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` now adds operator-path coverage for named `--subvol` and `--snapshot` selection through the mount open path, including mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior scoped to the selected tree. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now adds FUSE E2E coverage (`#[ignore]` requiring sudo) for subvolume/snapshot mount selection proving root scoping to selected tree and `NotFound` errors for missing subvolumes. |
 | ext4 inline data read | `fs/ext4/inline.c` | ✅ | `read_ext4_inline_data()` reads from inode extent_bytes + system.data xattr. `INLINE_DATA` removed from rejected features. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies real `OpenFs::read` behavior for both pure-`i_block` inline payloads and `system.data` xattr continuation payloads, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path ordinary reads plus near-EOF short reads for both storage layouts. |
 | ext4 indirect block addressing | `fs/ext4/inode.c` | ✅ | `resolve_indirect_block()` handles direct + single/double/triple indirect pointers. `EXTENTS` no longer required at mount. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies single/double indirect block resolution and I/O boundaries for non-extent inodes. |
-| ext4 FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE | `fs/ext4/extents.c` | ✅ | ZERO_RANGE mode (0x10) zeroes allocated blocks in range via write path. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies `OpenFs::fallocate` zeroes the requested middle range while preserving adjacent data and file size, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies the real FUSE path emits the `ext4_rw_fallocate_zero_range_zeroes_range` contract while preserving unaffected bytes. |
+| ext4 fallocate range operations | `fs/ext4/extents.c` | ✅ | ZERO_RANGE mode (0x10) zeroes allocated blocks in range via the write path. COLLAPSE_RANGE (0x08) removes a block-aligned interior range, shifts the tail left, shrinks `i_size`, and now rejects ranges that reach EOF with `EINVAL`; INSERT_RANGE (0x20) inserts a zero-reading block-aligned hole inside the file, shifts the tail right, and grows `i_size`. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies `OpenFs::fallocate` zero-range preservation, collapse shifting/shrinking plus EOF rejection with no mutation, and insert hole/growth behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies the real FUSE path emits the `ext4_rw_fallocate_zero_range_zeroes_range` contract while preserving unaffected bytes. |
 | ext4 fast commit replay | `fs/ext4/fast_commit.c` | ✅ | `replay_fast_commit()` in `ffs-journal` parses FC tag streams (HEAD/TAIL/INODE/ADD_RANGE/DEL_RANGE/CREAT/LINK/UNLINK/PAD), buffers operations until commit `TAIL`, and forces fallback when the stream is truncated or incomplete. `OpenFs` extracts deterministic fast-commit evidence and applies committed FC operations at mount time via `apply_fast_commit_operations()`. FC operations are processed after JBD2 replay: Create/Link/Unlink/AddRange/DelRange are logged as observational evidence (JBD2 replay provides the authoritative block-level recovery); InodeUpdate triggers a verification read to confirm the inode is accessible. Truncated or incomplete FC streams fall back to JBD2-only replay. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies committed fast-commit evidence extraction plus deterministic JBD2-only fallback for truncated FC streams, and `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ext4_fast_commit.rs` now coverage-fuzzes the raw fast-commit replay parser with golden-derived committed and truncated seeds. |
 | btrfs tree-log replay | `fs/btrfs/tree-log.c` | ✅ | `replay_tree_log()` in ffs-btrfs walks tree-log tree when `log_root != 0`, returns items for FS tree merge, and is wired into the mount path. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies multilevel log-tree replay through chunk mapping plus the zero-`log_root` fast path, and `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_btrfs_tree_log.rs` now coverage-fuzzes synthesized tree-log recovery over valid multilevel trees, absent log roots, checksum/structure failures, and equivalent chunk mappings. |
 | ext4 casefold (case-insensitive dirs) | `fs/ext4/namei.c` | ✅ | `lookup_in_dir_block_casefold()` with Unicode lowercase comparison. CASEFOLD removed from rejected features. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies real `OpenFs` lookup/readdir behavior on a CASEFOLD-flagged ext4 directory image. |
